item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: With the loss of the image and the lack of additional information regarding the D class (was this individual someone who previously disappeared or from a completely different site?) I'm going to have to down vote this. Shame the author isn't around any more to tweak it or even find a new image.

item.title: Staff Post - Open
item.content:encoded: The image that was used in this article is non-compliant with the SCP wiki's license (CC BY-SA 3.0) as the image comes from here and is All Rights Reserved. Thus, the image has been removed.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
This comment is really necro and I think the author is gone (account deleted on their comments) but something that bothers me is where the d class comes from.

Is he a test subject that previously disappeared? Or was he sent in there that night? Or do they not know how he got into the area?

Otherwise I kinda dig it.

Also, even though the author (I think) said it doesn't have to do with Christmas, it sure seems like light beings (stars?) Putting people up on trees as revenge :)

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I read this in the best time possible, the image is cool and the anomaly is beautifully nonsensical. Some really bad redaction in the sentence "It is heavily forested, and experiences snowfall ██% of the year."; but it is barely good enough for me to forgive that.
+1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Ohhhhh! I remember this SCP when I re-discovered the SCP wiki! This one really creeped me out and I enjoyed it. +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: It was completely 'meh' for me until I thought this: In soviet Canada, Christmas lights hung you up on the trees. I like it better now, but not enough as to upvote. In fact, I am a bit surprised about the large quantity of upvotes this has achieved.

item.title: Re: +100
item.content:encoded: Sure, feel free.

item.title: Re: +100
item.content:encoded: I've been wanting to actually give this one a bit of a rewrite, but now I feel like that'd just be a bad idea.

item.title: Re: +100
item.content:encoded:
Congrats Wilt! (As a side note can I just call you sensei now?)

Don't worry reject, you're not the only one.

Congrats! Very well-written, Wilt.

I still hate the picture though.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
He was a former D-Class who was sent into the zone for testing, and wound up re-appearing.

I'm not sure where you're getting this other stuff from.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I also would like it better if they weren't directly hostile towards humans. Indifferent would be great.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Was the D-class in the last incident actually "identified as D-39411", meaning we already had this guy as a D-class and somehow he wound up inside the zone? Or was he subsequently numbered as a D-class posthumously? The latter makes no sense, considering what D-class personnel are; and the former prompts the question of what prior connection he might have had with this SCP.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
CHRISTMAS WHY DO YOU HAUNT THIS SCP

I appreciate it.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I dug the atmosphere—the feeling of this pagan egregore created by our Christmas traditions. I heard jingle bells the whole time I was reading it.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Everything went better than expected.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Holy Sweet Spruces! I just got it.

Thank you, picklejuice. You've opened my eyes. I only saw 1358 as some weird not particularly interesting thing before.

Changing my neutral vote to an upvote.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Not all SCPs are meant to be horrifying - SCPs are put in place for anything that otherwise disrupts everyday normalcy.

Edited to change from tactless insult to coherent counter-argument.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Not everything has to be the next big horror show.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Christmas thing that kills people and puts them up as ornaments. Not scary.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Due to popular demand, I have uncensored the dates.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I like the idea, but the idea of blacking out just the first two digits of the year is dumb. Either black out the whole year or nothing.

Novote.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: This is so weird. I really like it. +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I want to know why the glow-folk do these things. The behaviors make no sense, but it feel like they might make sense in some internally-consistent, dream-logic way if only I knew more. Provoking fascinated curiosity gets a strong +1 from me.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Glowing stick figures that place you on top of trees? How delightfully random! It is nice to see an SCP that, though possibly dangerous, is not out to murder you.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I only used "hostile" because I didn't have any other word. :V They aren't necessary malicious at all.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I'm quite the opposite. Though perhaps the 'what it does to people to make them disappear' should not be described as 'hostile.' It's just something inexplicable the outlines do to people who enter the enclosure, and it may or may not be 'hostile' in a meaningful sense, even though the consequences (disappearance) are not what the people involved would want.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
"you can please some of the people…"

part of what makes this work is how short it is - just like 173. it's good you're not trying to please everyone, as you'd probably lose what makes it work so well if you did.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Nope, sorry.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I'm really glad a lot of people like this, but it does kinda suck that there's also a nice group of people who aren't too crazy about it. I guess that's the danger of trying to appeal using a single mechanic. But hey! People are loving it, and I love that, so I know the parts that I'm doing right. I also know what I can use some work on. Win-win.

And for those who don't really quite get it, I did have an idea as to why they do the thing with the trees, but you can always just PM me about that. I don't want to ruin it for everybody else.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: With Sorts here. It's kind of a letdown. Awesome picture, nice concept and then…people being stuck on trees.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I like it Wilt, and it gets my ennnhhh +1, but you should have taken it further. This article is missing something.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: This is kind of far-fetched, but does this have anything to do with Jacob's Ladder?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: This just doesn't do it for me. I mean, it does something to people. What? Why? We just aren't given enough information.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
It's a snazzy idea, to start with.
But I didn't quite get what they did to subjects, but it did seem festive.
Anyway, voted up from me, good stuff.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: agreed, also.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Which is lovely, yes, but there's still something gained in a downvote.

At least when it comes with criticism, anyways.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: This, all of it.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I'm pretty sure this is going to stick around anyway, people are digging it

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I agree that blacking out the first two digits in the year makes zero sense. That said, I like the entities themselves, and what they do is just fantastically bizarre. The list of anomalous behavior is great, too, if only because I can't figure out a single damn thing about these, and I love that.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Very nice use of this picture. Upvoted.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I agree with Sorts.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Aw, well, can't win 'em all. At least you liked the general idea. :D That gives me enough of an idea as to what I should try next.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I had to downvote because of what they do to people. It makes no sense in my head, and it doesn't scare me. But everything else is very good! I like the general idea, I'd probably prefer if this was mysterious and only incidentally dangerous rather than a human hunter.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I really like this! The picture is excellent and the article keeps up the quality.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
at 1930 a man in a red suit and beard was reported surrounded by 1358-1…….

+1 though, lols

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Glad I did it (decent) justice!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Decent job, Wilt. I like the ladders.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Nice to see that picture getting used.+1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Despite how hard I tried, I just couldn't make it any less Christmas-themed. Let's just say it's an early Christmas SCP.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: It's more a reference to canon. People have differing ideas of when the Foundation was founded, and when an SCP may be contained. It gives the reader a bit of freedom.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Not bad, but this does nothing for me at all. Seems like it's aiming for interesting via weirdness but just doesn't quite cut it. And all I can think of is Christmas lights.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Why only redact the first two digits of the year and nothing else? Is the plot twist supposed to be that the logs were made starting in 1892?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: As excellent as it was in sandbox, I am pleased to be able to upvote. Good work, Mr_Wilt.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: This has some grammar and tone issues, and the times thing could be more elegantly done by simply referring to hours elapsed since sunrise and until sun set rather than suggesting that the document is updated every few hours. I like the picture and the gimmick with the floodlights but don't fully understand what their effect is. No vote for now.
